title: Maximum Points You Can Obtain From Cards
hints: Consider the cards you do not pick. They form a single contiguous subarray of fixed length. Maximizing your score is equivalent to minimizing the sum of these unpicked cards.
Calculate the sum of the first n - k cards. This represents the initial window of unpicked cards. Then slide this window one position to the right at a time.
As you slide the window, subtract the element leaving the window and add the element entering it. Track the minimum sum encountered during this process to find the maximum possible score.
